source: National Association of Colleges and Employers, supplemented by Maine employer surveys

competencies.name: Critical Thinking
competencies.description: Identify and respond to needs based upon an understanding of situational context and logical analysis of relevant information
competencies.agent_notes: Assessed through problem-solving in exams and projects

competencies.name: Communication
competencies.description: Clearly and effectively exchange information, ideas, facts, and perspectives with persons inside and outside of an organization
competencies.agent_notes: Primary focus of CMJ 103; assessed through presentations and written work in other courses

competencies.name: Teamwork
competencies.description: Build and maintain collaborative relationships to work effectively toward common goals, while appreciating diverse viewpoints and shared responsibilities
competencies.agent_notes: Typically assessed through group projects; often underweighted in STEM courses

competencies.name: Technology
competencies.description: Understand and leverage technologies ethically to enhance efficiencies, complete tasks, and accomplish goals
competencies.agent_notes: CS and data science courses inherently address this; gen-ed courses may not

competencies.name: Career & Self-Development
competencies.description: Proactively develop oneself and one's career through continual personal and professional learning, awareness of one's strengths and weaknesses, navigation of career opportunities, and networking
competencies.agent_notes: PRIMARY ALIGNMENT TARGET for the agent — this competency is rarely explicitly addressed in courses but is the core of what the agent helps students develop

competencies.name: Equity & Inclusion
competencies.description: Demonstrate the awareness, attitude, knowledge, and skills required to equitably engage and include people from different local and global cultures
competencies.agent_notes: Addressed in communication courses through audience analysis; in data science through fairness/bias topics

competencies.name: Leadership
competencies.description: Recognize and capitalize on personal and team strengths to achieve organizational goals
competencies.agent_notes: Often implicit in group work; rarely explicitly taught or assessed

competencies.name: Professionalism
competencies.description: Knowing work environments differ greatly, understand and demonstrate effective work habits, and act in the interest of the larger community and workplace
competencies.agent_notes: Assessed through deadlines, academic integrity, and professional conduct
